sections.paragraph: and מֹורִיָּה Moriah, pr. name of a hill of Jerusalem, on which Solomon built the temple, 2Ch 3:1. Gen 22:2, אֶרֶץ הַמֹּרִיָּה “the land of Moriah,” i.e. the region around that mountain, its vicinity, as if district of Moriah; comp. אֶרֶץ הָעַי Jos 8:1. As to the origin, the sacred writers themselves ( Gen 22:8, 14 Gen 22:142 Chron. loc. cit.) make allusion to the etymology from the root רָאָה, which is confirmed by regarding מֹרִיָּה as contr. from מָרְאִי־יָהּ for מָרְאֵה יָהּ (Part. Ho.), with Yod of union, i.e. chosen by Jehovah, a name which is very suitable for a sanctuary.
